label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's break this down. The question is asking whether Brown intentionally rolled a six. Brown wants to kill Smith and has a bomb wired to detonate if a six is shown on a camera lens. Brown tosses a fair six-sided die hoping it will land six-up. A six is rolled, the bomb detonates, and Smith dies. We need to consider what it means to "intentionally roll a six." Intention requires that the person had a specific purpose or goal to achieve a particular outcome. Brown's goal is to kill Smith. He knows that rolling a six will result in the bomb detonating and killing Smith. He uses the die because he wants the outcome of rolling a six. He hopes it will land six-up. So, his intention is to achieve the six outcome because it leads to Smith's death. Even though rolling a die is a probabilistic event with chance involved, Brown is using it as a means to an end. He is intentionally trying to get a six because he knows that's the only way to detonate the bomb. So, yes, he is intentionally rolling a six. To rephrase: Brown's intention to kill Smith is what drives him to roll the die in the hope that it lands six-up.
